title: Stan Arts
text: Home * People * Stan Arts Stan Arts and Richard Pijl @ PT 48 [1] Stan Arts , a Dutch computer game and chess programmer, who already started programming with age eight on a Commodore 64 , writing programs and games in Basic and 6502 assembly language, since 1994 on an 486 PC in Turbo Pascal and inline assembly with focus on 3D graphics . Contents 1 Chess Programming 2 Forum Posts 2.1 2002 ... 2.2 2010 ... 3 External Links 4 References Chess Programming [ edit ] In 2002, Stan Arts became interested in chess programming, starting with an 8x8 Board and re-inventing Minimax . Stan discovered chess programming in the Internet, learned about alpha-beta and met José Lauro Strapasson [2] , author of PreChess , who pointed him the Free-Pascal compiler and taught him the WinBoard protocol [3] . Already in summer 2002 Stan was able to publish his first WinBoard compatible chess engine dubbed Stan's Chess [4] , soon also with an own 3D Graphics Board GUI , later evolving to Neurosis . His recent program Nemeton , also written in Pascal, had its debut at the PT 47 in 2014, and was released soon after [5] . Forum Posts [ edit ] 2002 ...
